Action Against Hunger serves more than 26.5 million people in more than 55 countries. Our team works across the world to ensure everyone can access clean water, food, training, and healthcare. Together, we extensively research global crises, implement innovative and sustainable solutions, provide critical care to malnourished children, and work to create a hunger-free world.

Healthcare Remote Jobs Supporting Patient Care From Anywhere

If you’re a healthcare professional considering a change, remote jobs might offer you the flexibility you’ve been seeking. Working from home now extends far beyond traditional roles, from telehealth appointments to behind-the-scenes operations. You could find yourself making a meaningful impact on patient care—no matter your location. With technology opening new doors, you’ll soon discover how these roles are shaping the future of the industry and what that could mean for you next.

The Evolution of Remote Work in Healthcare

The landscape of healthcare employment has undergone notable changes in recent years, particularly in the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic. As one explores job opportunities or joins talent communities within the sector, there is an observable increase in available remote positions across various roles, such as Clinical Analysts and software engineers. This shift reflects healthcare organizations' efforts to deliver services to patients regardless of geographic location.

The move towards remote work in healthcare presents opportunities for career advancement, including various job openings and internship programs. These roles often incorporate a level of flexibility that can enhance work-life balance while still adhering to stringent standards for personal information protection and regulatory compliance.

Careers
Careers in Massage Therapy at Align

Align was founded in 2000 and has organically grown to a group of 8 locations in Ottawa.  After 25 years, we've got the business side down pat, and welcome new RMTs and experienced ones to join us and lose the stress. 




We have everything you require to start, grow and maintain a practice - receptionists, linen service, marketing - social media and radio ads, online booking and charting, tools and training and a competitive split. 

We don't have crappy pay (We love writing big cheques!), rigid scheduling, micromanagers or scary contracts. Most therapists start and stay with us, and move on if they literally move (to another city usually). 




Align is by far the largest employer of massage therapists in the city (1/5), and with good reason. We are a team, a group of friends and would love to meet you!




Currently, we are looking for full or part-time RMTs for Kanata, Rockland, Bank St, Barrhaven, Lindenlea, and Sandy Hill. 




Are you an RMT looking to open a clinic and unsure where to start? We are always looking for keeners to help grow the brand, so please reach out and we can show you how much easier it is working in a team.


Please send a resume, or pop in for a visit! You can reach the head honcho at jana@alignyourbody.com

The Alpine Club of Canada (ACC) was founded in 1906 and is a not for profit, charitable amateur athletic association with approximately 10,000 members throughout Canada and around the world. Incorporated in 1909 by a legislative act of Alberta known as The Alpine Club of Canada Act, we maintain charitable status in Canada and the United States.

The company offers competitive wages for tailors with opportunities for advancement in the trade.
Immigrant Tailors
Since there is no trade school teaching tailoring in Canada, most of our tailors are immigrants that have become Canadian citizens. In fact, we most likely have more European-trained master tailors on staff than any other tailoring facility in Canada. We have tailors mostly from Romania, Ukraine, Syria and India nevertheless, we seek tailors from any country who have a tradition of training tailors.

Reporting to the Senior Director, Clinical Impact & Performance, you are an independent primary care provider who is responsible for delivering evidence-based, patient-centered services to people with arthritis in the GTA (including Etobicoke & Downtown Toronto). This is accomplished both through in-person and virtual modes of service delivery.

What We Do:

Arthritis Society Canada is on a mission to fight the fire of arthritis with the fire of research, innovation, advocacy and information and support. That’s because arthritis robs six million Canadians of their mobility, mental and physical well-being, and in some cases, their livelihood. It is Canada’s most common chronic condition, and there is no cure.

A culture with purpose

Working at Atuka means contributing everyday to the development trajectories of potentially life-changing therapeutics, supported by world-class facilities and scientists, and an industry-leading benefits plan.

Atuka offers a way of doing science that is different from traditional contract research organizations.

Every day, we support our partners in making informed, critical decisions at the interface between the preclinical and the clinical, ensuring that future research and investment is directed toward the avenues with the best chance of success.

At the same time, our culture remains very much influenced by our roots in academic research and experimentation, and we are committed to ongoing scientific discovery in Parkinson’s and other disorders.

LIFE AT ATUKA

Atuka is a place for scientists who want their work to matter. See how we work, grow, and collaborate to advance the next generation of Parkinson’s therapies.

Download our Culture Book
At Atuka we embrace our role in nurturing and preparing the next generation of scientists, technicians, and support teams for the crucial challenges that lay ahead. 

Work alongside a team of leading neuroscientists with numerous research breakthroughs to their credit.

Join a culture that values collaboration, scientific inquiry, and continuous learning.

Define the career path most meaningful, interesting, and rewarding to you.

Careers and Articling Opportunities at MGL Lawyers
At MGL Lawyers, we value giving articling students hands-on practical experience to set them up for success in their legal careers. Our articling program is directly supervised by our lawyers and you will have exposure to the entire team and various legal matters.
Why Article at MGL Lawyers?

As an articling student, you will be exposed to a wide range of immigration matters. There will also be opportunities to gain exposure in business law and in litigation matters. You will work with our team of lawyers in drafting, preparing immigration applications, legal opinions, attending meetings with our lawyers, and contributing to strategy.  

Our senior lawyers are dedicated to providing mentorship, guidance and support, helping you develop skills and confidence throughout your articling term.

Our collaborative approach and inclusive environment are at the heart of everything we do. You’ll work closely with lawyers and our team throughout your articling term.

We offer opportunities for training, development and networking, including workshops, seminars and legal community events.

Who are we looking for?

The firm seeks graduates and students who are resourceful, have a go-getter attitude, and can work in a collaborative team environment. An interest and passion for immigration is considered an asset when applying to our team.

Thank you for your interest in working at the BC Centre for Disease Control.
Facilities


BCCDC's main facility is located at 655 West 12th Avenue in Vancouver. This facility is state-of-the-art and was built in 1997. Services at the main facility are augmented by street nurse and outreach nurse services and offices in the inner city of Vancouver, by an ambulatory clinic for tuberculosis in New Westminster and by consultative clinics at various locations around the province.  

Our mission

Our mission is leadership in disease control through learning and action vision. We believe that there are no boundaries to what we can do to better the health of British Columbians. Therefore, each team member of BCCDC dedicates energy and expertise to the prevention, detection and cure of disease. We lead through innovation and action.   

Professional development

BCCDC provides its employees with the training and career development necessary to serve its clients, meet accreditation standards, remain current with scientific information and standards and respond to changing market forces. 

Our work environment

BCCDC provides a healthy, safe and rewarding work environment, which fosters employee trust, growth and development.

Home Education & Careers
Education & Careers

Professional Development is critical to anyone in the workforce.
It is often asked what is the importance of continuing professional development and why does it matter?

When you have finished your degree, completed practical experience requirements and started your career, it’s fair to say the first part of your mission is well and truly accomplished. Sit back and give yourself a pat on the back. But don’t take too long doing it or you’ll be lagging behind your colleagues. The same is true for professionals with many years of experience in the workplace.

Continuing professional development is important because it ensures you continue to be competent in your profession. It is an ongoing process and continues throughout a professional’s career.

The ultimate outcome of well-planned continuing professional development is that it safeguards the public, the employer, the professional and the professional’s career.

ABOUT CMH

Campbellford Memorial Hospital (CMH) is a 38-bed health care facility located in Trent Hills Ontario. We serve approximately 40,000 Northumberland, Peterborough and Hastings County residents, as well as a large seasonal population of cottagers and tourists enjoying the beautiful Kawartha Lakes Region and the Trent River System.

We  provide a comprehensive array of acute care services including:

4-Bed Special Care Unit
Endoscopy Surgical Suite
Diagnostic Imaging Department
Laboratory
Numerous Out-Patient Clinics
24/7 Emergency Department
Mental Health Clinic
Geriatric Assessment and Intervention Network (GAIN)
Supportive Housing.

We have a highly engaged team of staff, physicians and volunteers who are dedicated to making CMH a great place to work and receive care. We currently have more than 260 people including administrators, nurses, physicians, allied health professionals and students who serve our community by contributing their invaluable skills and dedication each and every day. If you are interested in providing high quality care to patients and families and becoming part of a tight knit Hospital community, we want you to be part of the team!

What it's like to work at Campbell Scientific Canada

CURRENT JOB OPPORTUNITIES

All around us, decisions are made that affect our world. At Campbell Scientific Canada, we take pride in knowing we make a difference to the environment and to the people we care about. At CSC we help scientists, CEOs, and even politicians make informed choices based on strong, science-based data derived from our top-quality instrumentation. We provide technology, expertise, and passion for the environment. Our people are driven, fun-loving, and caring individuals. We endeavor as a team and work for what matters to us.

It is rewarding to work for a company that has a positive impact on society and the environment. Located in Edmonton, Campbell Scientific (Canada) Corp. (CSC) employs over 75 people dedicated to delivering state-of-the-art measurement and control systems to customers all over the world. CSC offers competitive compensation packages coupled with an emphasis on work-life balance.

Join our team at Community Renewal!

We’re a passionate team dedicated to fostering community development and renewal across Canada. Our team spans the country, collaborating closely with community foundations and partners nationwide.

At Community Renewal, we believe in dreaming big and making a real impact. Our culture is built on support, collaboration, and a strong commitment to teamwork. We value diversity in all its forms — from geographic backgrounds to education, culture, ethnicity, Indigenous status, and beyond. We celebrate the unique perspectives and lived experiences that each team member brings to the table.

Joining our team means being part of a community-focused organization where your voice matters. We are committed to creating a future where everyone feels they belong, starting with our inclusive and supportive workplace.

Whether you’re passionate about community engagement, advocacy, or project management, Community Renewal offers opportunities for growth and learning.

If you’re ready to make a difference and contribute to meaningful change in communities across Canada, we invite you to explore our current career opportunities. Together, we can build a future where every community thrives.

Join us in shaping a better tomorrow at Community Renewal!

Keep an eye out here for any open job opportunities with the Child Welfare League of Canada!

Camille Breton Skagen

Operations Manager

Camille holds a Master in Sociology from the University of British Columbia and has completed a wide array of professional development training, with a focus on effective leadership, conflict resolution and communication. An experienced project manager for a diverse range of startups and community based projects, Camille most recently served as project manager for a large scale clinical checklist initiative. Run in association with the Ontario Incidence Study of Reported Child Abuse and Neglect, the project responded to the needs of child welfare-involved families during the COVID-19 pandemic. Since 2020, Camille has been the project manager at the League for both the Beyond Neglect project and the Strength of Families and Connection initiative. She is known for her innovative vision, her leadership skills and her facility in building strong working relationships.

Number of Positions Available: 2 

 

Anticipated start date: July 6, 2026 

 

End date: August 28, 2026

​

Deadline to apply: May 15, 2026 

 

How to apply: Send a resume and cover letter, in one document, should be emailed to susanlane@annapolisriver.ca 

 

Job Description/Responsibilities: 

  

The Ecological Field Technician will provide support for a range of programs for the Clean Annapolis River Project aimed at supporting ecosystem health, biodiversity and climate resilience in southwest Nova Scotia through collaboration with the agricultural sector, government partners, academic institutions, community organizations, and individual residents. The Ecological Field Technician will be responsible for assisting with both field and office-related work and will need to be able to complete physically demanding outdoor work in varying and unpredictable weather (e.g., rain, heat, cold) and terrains (forests, wetlands, watercourses).

Meal preparation and cooking
Conversation and companionship

A career with Community Living Welland Pelham
Providing Support

For 70 years Community Living Welland Pelham has been dedicated to offering supports and services to people who have an intellectual disability and their families.   We are committed to providing supports based on a vision of “an inclusive caring community where all people belong and have equal opportunity to participate effectively”.  We are always looking for dynamic, enthusiastic employees who share this vision.

Your Role

As an employee with Community Living Welland Pelham, you will be expected to work within a team environment. We are looking for strong candidates that will take an active role in supporting people to achieve their personal goals, facilitate relationships, build skills and valued social roles. This is done by building relationships in our community, coordinating services and offering individualized supports.

We hire for entry level support workers (part-time hours)
on an ongoing basis

À propos de la CAPSA

Nous sommes l'un des 40 organismes de bassins versants reconnu par le gouvernement du Québec et un OBNL qui soutient, conseille, agit et harmonise par la concertation, la gestion intégrée de l’eau du bassins versant des rivières Sainte-Anne, Portneuf et du secteur La Chevrotière.

Notre mission est d'offrir notre expertise à la communauté afin d'harmoniser les activités humaines aux enjeux liés à l'eau et ses écosystèmes.

Underlying new technologies is increasingly complicated and connected software, often involving AI and machine learning. How can innovators be sure their technology works only as intended? Critical Systems Labs Inc. (CSL) works to find the unexpected before it happens.

We are a collaborative team of intelligent and creative people, internationally recognized for our expertise in the management of safety risk and security vulnerabilities associated with complex software intensive systems. We work on interesting and technically advanced projects in aerospace, automotive, energy, maritime, medical, and rail signalling.


CSL has generous benefits package and paid vacation. We work on a hybrid model that is mostly remote. We have an accessible office located in Waterfront Station, the central transit hub in downtown Vancouver, Canada. Secured indoor bike parking is available at our office.

At CSL we value each member of our team and the contributions they make. We share knowledge so we are all constantly learning and so that we consistently produce the highest quality solutions. As the province’s largest Rehabilitation and Long Term Care facility, Deer Lodge Centre provides many opportunities for challenging experiences and career growth. Conveniently located on Portage Avenue, only a short walk from Assiniboine Park, DLC inpatient and outpatient programming includes Rehabilitation, Respiratory, Mental Health, Chronic and Personal Care. As well, we maintain a proud over 100-year tradition of serving Veterans and the military through inpatient and outpatient programs including our Operational Stress Injury Clinic. As an operating division of the Winnipeg Regional Health Authority, Deer Lodge Centre serves as a centre of excellence in rehabilitation and long term care for the region and provides many regional support services.

The Centre’s motto of “Making Lives Better” applies to the staff as well as the patients and residents. Broad educational offerings, an active Wellness Committee and on-site fitness facility, and access to research are just some of what we offer to support staff in their career and personal lives.

The Diefenbunker is a one-of-a-kind museum and national historic site. Our unique workplace inside a historic Cold War bunker and our passionate and inclusive team make for a fulfilling working environment. Be part of a team that is preserving and interpreting important history for present and future generations. 

We occasionally have employment opportunities open to the public. Positions can range from guides and project coordinators, to marketing, development, and management roles. If you enjoy history, working with enthusiastic people, and live in the Ottawa area, we invite you to consider applying to join our team at the Diefenbunker!

 MEET OUR TEAM
 VOLUNTEER OPPORTUNITIES
SUPPORT OUR MISSION

We are a one-of-a-kind museum and national historic site, operating as an independent charitable organization. Your support enables us to continue preserving important history and educating present and future generations on the Cold War in Canada.

Embedded Access is a young, dynamic company that puts the fun back into a career! We strive to maintain a stimulating and challenging environment that rewards innovation and each individual’s contribution. Each member of our team plays a critical role in our success and contributes to our future growth.

We are always interested in hearing from people with various skills. Feel free to contact us regarding roles you might be interested in fulfilling.

Sound understanding of various processor architectures.

Everything we do is to empower our customers to excel at their jobs, while continuing to challenge ourselves to grow and improve every day.

Every lawyer in Canada and notary in Quebec is required by law to be a member of a law society and to be governed by its rules.

Overview
What is the Federation of Law Societies of Canada?

The Federation of Law Societies of Canada is the national association of the 14 law societies mandated by the provinces and territories to regulate Canada’s legal profession in the public interest. It is the body through which Canada’s law societies collaborate at the national level, share information on trends and issues affecting the legal profession, and engage in collective action and decision-making. The Federation is also the law societies’ national and international voice on important issues related to the regulation and core values of the legal profession.

Serving the Public Interest
Leading the Development of High Standards of Regulation
Leader in Free Access to Legal Information
Certifying Body of Internationally Educated Lawyers and Law Graduates
Provider of National Continuing Legal Education Programs
National Voice of Canada’s Law Societies
Our Vision

Acting in the public interest by strengthening Canada’s system of governance of an independent legal profession, reinforcing public confidence in it and making it a leading example for justice systems around the world.

“Career Quest: Real Stories, Real Success” is a dynamic and educational program that inspires and informs students about different career pathways through real life stories of professionals. The program invites guest speakers from a range of industries to visit classrooms and share their personal journeys, career trajectories, and current job insights.

Career Quest enriches students’ learning experiences by connecting them with real world professionals. By hearing firsthand accounts of success and exploring diverse career paths, students gain motivation and valuable insights that help them navigate their own paths to future success.

Inspiration and Motivation

Showcase the remarkable success stories of professionals who have reached significant career milestones.

Emphasize the importance of education, skill development, and lifelong learning in achieving career goals.

Archiving Goulbourn:

The Museum is enhancing the accessibility of its collections by transcribing historic handwritten documents and manuscripts.

Our current project focuses on a Goulbourn Township Council meeting minutes book, with records dating back to 1899. This document holds special significance, as it was originally written in the very building that now houses the Museum, which once served as the Township Hall.

This volunteer opportunity is fully remote and hosted through the Museum’s Google Workspace account. Volunteers are given access to a shared folder containing transcription assignments and use a Google Sheets template to record the transcribed text.

As an Archiving Goulbourn volunteer, you can set your own schedule, contributing as much time as you wish to this meaningful preservation project.

For more information about this volunteer opportunity please email info@goulbournmuseum.ca

So YOU want to join Hardcore Gamer? Cool, but hold your Eponas. While we’re always looking for talented gamers and journalists to join the team, we actually value your gaming knowledge more than your Twitter followers. Shocking, we know. Played Mario with your brother growing up and now write about video games after quitting a music blog? Don’t apply. Loved Rez enough that you imported a trance vibrator from Japan? We want you! If you’re a strong, passionate writer with a unique voice and were a gamer before you even thought about being a journalist, we’ll get together like chocolate and peanut butter.

In the over ten years we’ve been in publication, we’ve had every conceivable type of writer come through and have learned one thing: resumé appearance can be deceiving. Someone may have graduated from Cornell (besides Andy Bernard) yet have no idea how to handle writing about video games in a real world setting. Our staff have been through the trenches, around for years or even decades writing about video games not because they’re trying to be the next personality, but because they simply love games. With 4,000 employees, 600 highly skilled medical staff and scientists, 2,100 learners and 700 volunteers, we are one of the largest employers in the region and the second largest hospital designated under Ontario’s French Language Services Act. 

As the tertiary care centre with 25 sites across the region, we provide critical and inpatient care for more than half a million people across a variety of services and programs including cardiac care, oncology, nephrology, trauma and rehabilitation. 

We are one of Canada’s research hospitals, and our Regional Cancer Program ranks among the top five in Ontario on quality improvement indicators. 

We are an organization dedicated to being patient and family focused, digitally enabled, and socially accountable, while supporting and developing our people and advancing our academic and research impact for Northeastern Ontario.

WHY WORK FOR INFINITY NETWORK SOLUTIONS?

At Infinity Network Solutions, we believe that great technology starts with great people. We're more than an IT provider—we're a team that values collaboration, personal growth, and making a real difference for our clients every day. If you're looking for a workplace where your contributions matter and your teammates have your back, you'll feel right at home.

CARE

We genuinely care about our clients, our teammates, and the work we do. That means taking ownership, supporting one another, and going the extra mile to ensure every solution is delivered with quality and compassion. We believe that people come first, and technology is simply the tool that helps us serve them better.

RESPECT

Respect is the foundation of every relationship we build. We listen to different perspectives, communicate openly, and treat everyone with professionalism and kindness. We're Building Something Real

Our Team is Building the Future, Remotely and On-Site

We’re engineers, gamer geeks, aviation nerds, and drone enthusiasts. The individuals who are building the future here have travelled many paths - but what we all have in common is the ability to thrive on change; a desire to work at something meaningful; and an unquenchable need to invent the future in our own image. This is Kongsberg Geospatial.


Current Job Listings

We Build Meaningful Technology


Kongsberg Geospatial creates real-time geospatial technology that powers life-protecting defense systems and unmanned aerial systems worldwide - systems that do such things as deliver defibrillators to people in need and help search and rescue operations in the Arctic.

People rely on our technology for a wide range of applications including air defense, search and rescue, drone delivery systems, and autonomous vehicle tracking.

Our team members make a real impact in people's lives.


We’re expanding our team, and we’re looking for people who want to leave their mark on the world.
‍


Take Control of Your Work Environment

Our team members have the opportunity for real input into product direction. Our engineers enjoy technical autonomy and get to design the software they build. We create high-performance technology with real-world applications. Join us, and make a difference.


Kongsberg Geospatial is a fully owned subsidiary of Kongs

We believe that childhood is  a crucial time when we get emotional and cognitive foundations. We would like to help children in their development  to become long-life learners and responsible, well-adjusted individuals.

We trust that we may accomplish this through:

Providing a warm, stable environment that ensures the basic needs of home – creative, positive play, good discipline, interacting with peers, closeness to nature, nutritious food, rest and a lot of individual attention that all will guarantee a sense of security and ensure good contact with daily life.
Creating a partnership with parentsand the community to continuously guide children’s learning. We trust that every parent, child and teacher at Little Explorers will discover joy on the path of learning as he or she challenges, leads and nurtures one another in a creative, natural environment.
Maintaining a carefully prepared learning environment with full of educational Reggio Emilia materials.
Creating awareness of each child’s unique development and personality, therefore we support children’s different ways of learning and communicating in an atmosphere that is full of respect and understanding.

At Lokma, we focus on the freshness and uniqueness of our products. We advocate the family values of sharing; and consider these to be the ingredients that guide us in this common adventure with you.

The idea behind Lokma began after a trip to Turkey where the founders discovered a traditional Turkish pastry called Lokma. From the first bite of this little delight, they fell head over heels in love. In addition to being able to extend this wonderful culture through this dessert, the founders have developed a comprehensive menu in which everyone can find it’s ideal taste.

As our company continues to grow, we find ourselves connecting with incredible individuals like you who are eager to contribute to our success story.

We want to shed light on our hiring process and clarify a crucial aspect – we do not recruit employees via third-parties or recruiters. Work Environment:

With tremendous growth and diversification since 1970, McAsphalt has maintained a strong belief in family and a tight-knit environment where people have the opportunity to excel through strong relationships in a team-based atmosphere.

Tools:

Our employees are provided the tools they need to be an expert in whatever field they are in. McAsphalt’s development and training programs ensure new employees get up to speed and existing employees are continually developing skills. This ensures success internally and is reflected in our exceptional customer service.

Communication:

Ideas and opinions are important at McAsphalt, as a company we proactively focus on communication. We understand that all employees can contribute to our overall success. Senior Leasing Manager (Commercial Real Estate)

Position Summary

Reporting to the SVP, Leasing, the Senior Leasing Manager is responsible for driving leasing performance across a diversified portfolio of retail and mixed-use assets, including fund-owned investments, core income-producing properties, and select development projects.

The role focuses on maximizing asset value through strategic leasing, tenant curation, and disciplined deal execution, while supporting both portfolio-level performance and investor and ownership objectives where applicable.

This position operates at the intersection of leasing, asset management, and investment strategy, ensuring leasing decisions enhance income stability, long-term value, and overall portfolio performance across different ownership structures.

This position offers exposure to both fund-driven investments and directly managed operating assets, providing a broader and more hands-on leasing platform.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and execute leasing strategies aligned with asset business plans and portfolio and investment performance targets

Source, negotiate, and complete lease transactions that enhance asset value and long-term income stability

Structure deals to optimize net effective rent, tenant quality, and risk-adjusted returns

The success of our system is our people, the owners of locations, and our local staff that support them. We are headquartered in Long Island, NY where our company began over 50 years ago.

Positions with our Franchise Locations

Owners throughout the world are regularly looking for great staff. If you are seeking a new career in the world of graphics, printing, marketing and communication technology, please contact us. With over 1000 locations, positions are available.

Dr. Thomas has guided scientists and engineers commercializing their inventions for social impact for many years. He teaches in the Invention to Innovation (i2I) program at Beedie and has taught Managing Technological Innovation in the SFU Management of Technology (MOT) MBA program.

Live to Play Sports is actively monitoring the progression of the coronavirus, COVID-19, to ensure that we are following updates and procedures from the government and public health. Any offer of employment will be made conditional upon the person providing proof of full COVID-19 vaccination or proof of an applicable exemption under human rights law.

For over 50 years, we have been a leading player in the Canadian and international bicycle market. Starting as a one-man operation in small-town British Columbia, we have always stayed true to our roots, which has helped us grow. We now have three international offices, two distribution centers, and more than 100 dedicated, fantastic staff that contribute to our continued success in the cycling industry.

We work hard, but we ride hard too. At Live to Play Sports, we have bicycle storage, showers, and lockers to encourage riding to, from, and at work. There is almost always someone getting out for a pedal, whether it is road, mountain, dirt jump, or a cruise. Medicine

Requirements : The Orleans Urgent care Clinic (OUCC) employs physicians with a fellowship or certification in Emergency Medicine or physicians with sufficient experience in emergency medicine. Because we are a fully equipped emergency facility, our physicians must have the appropriate training and experience. Many of our physicians continue to work in local Emergency Departments.

Employment Arrangement : Physicians may become OUCC associates however there are  also non associate physicians who work casual hours.

Hours : Physician weekday work hours are 8 a.m. to 3 p.m.,  9 a.m. to 4 p.m., and 3 p.m. to 9 p.m. Weekend hours are 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., and 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. The OUCC clinic is closed on statutory holidays.

Salaries : Physicians bill OHIP for insured services, and standard OMA rates are billed for uninsured services.

Nursing

Requirements : In keeping with the Orleans Urgent care Clinic’s high standard of emergency care, we hire experienced Registered Nurses (RN) and Registered Practical Nurses (RPN).

Employment Arrangement : Scheduling is managed by the Clinic’s nursing team leader. Schedules are distributed months in advance.

Hours : Weekday nursing hours are from 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., and from 3:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. Weekend hours are from 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.

Salaries : OUCC nurses are paid competitive hourly rates.

About ORC

The Ottawa Rowing Club (ORC) is a not-for-profit sports organization established in 1867 to provide rowing instruction for participants of all ages.  The ORC is comprised of a volunteer Executive Committee, volunteers and paid employees. 

The ORC provides coaching to rowers in competitive or sport rowing programs, as well as introductions to the sport through Discover Rowing Youth Summer Camps, Adult Learn-to-Row, and the Adult Rowing League.  Local high schools and universities train and race out of the ORC.

Our Club is located at 10 Lady Grey Dr, in the heart of Downtown Ottawa. Our Club is composed of 2 large boathouses, that include an indoor fitness area, offices, board room, boat storage, a ballroom, kitchenette, washrooms and change rooms. The Club is right on the edge of the Ottawa river with impressive panoramic views blending rural rugged shoreline with modern urban architecture.

At ORC we work in support of a diverse and equal world. We welcome all diverse people into our already eclectic teams. We encourage People of Colour, Indigenous Peoples, people from the LGBTQ+ community as well as people with Disabilities to apply!

Employment Opportunities

Every summer the Pacific Salmon Commission hires temporary staff in a variety of positions. There are opportunities for temporary full and part-time seasonal staff to work as test fishing observers on the Fraser River and in the Strait of Juan de Fuca.

The Stock ID group hires seasonal fishery samplers and the Commission’s scale lab at the Vancouver office hires a temporary lab assistant.

In addition, up to 10 staff are hired annually as seasonal fisheries technicians and boat operators to work at the PSC’s hydroacoustic fish counting station near Mission, BC which operates 24 hours a day during the fishing season.

Permanent, full-time positions become available on a much less frequent basis.

Peacekeeping Career: How to Start and What to Expect

Want a career that matters? Peacekeeping combines real-world impact with tough challenges. If you're curious about what's needed and how to begin, this page gives clear steps, real roles, and practical tips.

What peacekeeping jobs look like

Peacekeeping jobs vary. You can be a military officer, police officer, medic, logistician, civil affairs officer, human rights officer, or translator. Many missions also need engineers, IT specialists, and administrators. Civilian roles include political affairs, rule-of-law advisors, and community liaison officers. Short-term contract work and long-term positions both exist.

Skills and qualifications

Employers look for relevant education plus field experience. A degree in international relations, law, public health, engineering, or logistics helps. Practical experience through NGOs, national militaries, or police forces is very valuable. Learn a UN language: French, Spanish, Arabic, Russian, or Chinese can make you stand out, but English is usually required. Take courses in first aid, conflict analysis, protection of civilians, and humanitarian training. Mental resilience, cultural sensitivity, and basic medical knowledge matter as much as formal degrees.

Start with self-assessment: list your skills, languages, and physical readiness. Volunteer locally with refugee centers, NGOs, or disaster relief groups to get hands-on experience. Apply for short-term m

Pocket-lint is the website for entertainment technology, covering the vast world of mobile phones, tablets, laptops, TVs, streaming services, video games, and much more. If you want to know more about that screen you carry around in your pocket or bag, or the one in your living room, we’re always ready to help with advice and opinions.

Indeed, we’ve been doing so since 2003 - long before the iPhone was even a twinkle in Steve Jobs’ eye. And we’re pretty good at it too, having grown to be one of the most respected purveyors of tech news, tips, and reviews in the industry. You’ll often find a Pocket-lint logo or review score on packaging and company websites.

While our access to the likes of Apple and Google often results in in-depth time with the latest iPhones, Pixel handsets, and even Nintendo’s anticipated Switch 2.

In short, we’re proud of what we’ve achieved so far and, with your help, maybe we can reach even higher ground.

All applications for any position must be submitted through our online application process.  No paper copies will be accepted, unless otherwise specified.

CAREERS AT LUMENUS

Lumenus offers a broad range of high quality mental health, developmental, autism and early years intervention services to children, youth, families, and adults across Toronto. We offer a dynamic and enriching work environment, one that provides opportunities for growth and personal fulfillment. As a team we work together to ensure every Lumunus client has the support they need to be seen, be heard, be well. 

Lumenus Community Services is strongly committed to a workforce that reflects the diversity of the populations we serve. We encourage applications from all qualified individuals including applicants from all cultures, racialized communities, abilities, diverse sexual and gender identities and others who may contribute to the further diversification of ideas. We are committed to a selection process and work environment that is inclusive and barrier free.

Interested in joining the Lumenus team?

Click here for current job opportunities.


Accommodation Requests

We ask applicants to make any accommodation requests for the interview or selection process known in advance, by contacting the Human Resources Department. The Middlesex Hospital Alliance (MHA) is comprised of two fully accredited partner sites: Four Counties Health Services and Strathroy Middlesex General Hospital. Our Why = Your Why

We think our beliefs are the same as yours—that all are worthy of connectedness, companionship, and community.

What Is an Intellectual Disability?

An intellectual disability begins before adulthood and is a significantly reduced ability to:

Understand new or complex information
Learn and apply new skills

Signs of the disability often appear during childhood. It is a lifelong experience, with most people needing some type of assistance throughout their lives to live in and be part of their community.

Disability Service Sector Goals

We are aiming to raise awareness about the Disability Service sector and its workforce of over 4600 employees supporting 6500 people experiencing intellectual disabilities across Saskatchewan. It’s about getting to know us and being part of ensuring inclusive communities for people experiencing disabilities, and it all starts with saying hello.

The Disability Service sector’s ultimate goal is for people experiencing intellectual disabilities to live healthy, safe, and meaningful lives in their community.

Importance of Community Support

People experiencing intellectual disabilities often require support as part of daily life.

The amount of support depends on the extent of the disability
Some people may need to learn skills to live independently
Others may require 24/7 one-on-one support

Employment with Children’s Treatment Network

Children’s Treatment Network (CTN) supports approximately 42,000 kids and youth with disabilities and other developmental needs and their families. We provide intake, service navigation and coordinated service planning, assessment and diagnostic services, specialized clinics and rehabilitation services, including physiotherapy, occupational therapy and speech language therapy.

Funded by the Ministry of Children, Community and Social Services, CTN offers services through contracted partner organizations in the education, health and community sectors. Through its innovative network model, CTN’s commitment to providing family-centred care is anchored by an electronic client record that is shared across its partner organizations and provides the foundation for integrated plans of care and services.

Dedicated to creating a vibrant community where all kids, youth and families belong, we have a strong commitment to equity, diversity, inclusion, Indigeneity and accessibility (EDIIA). With this in mind, our goal is a workforce that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome all qualified applicants to apply including individuals with disabilities, those who identify as Black, Indigenous or persons of colour, members of the LGBTQ2S+ community and others. Careers at CMHA: Work With Us

The Canadian Mental Health Association (CMHA) is the most established, most extensive community mental health organization in Canada providing a full scope of mental health and addictions services for over 100 years. As the nationwide leader and champion for mental health, we facilitate access to the resources people require to maintain and improve mental health and community integration, build resilience, and support recovery from mental illness and addiction.

CMHA York Region South Simcoe (CMHA-YRSS) is one of the fastest growing branches of the Canadian Mental Health Association with around 350 staff and 100 volunteers. We offer 30+ publicly funded programs and services for youth (12+), adults and family caregivers. Last year we provided mental health and addictions services to more than 17,000 individuals.

Our community is filled with innovators who create change, professionals who deliver excellence, influencers who create impact, and passionate, determined people who are creating our vision of Mental Health for All.

The Highlands are an outdoor enthusiast’s dream each season of the year. From swimming, boating, fishing, and hiking in the summer, to cross country, downhill skiing, and snowmobiling in the winter, it’s all in your own backyard in the Highlands!

Who We Are

AbleLiving is a not for profit organization operated by Thrive Group. Thrive Group has been created to bring like-minded organizations together to develop a broad range and continuum of services that enable clients to live fulfilling lives as independently as possible.

Who We Hire

At Thrive Group organizations, workplace culture matters. We believe that providing our employees with a supportive, healthy work environment means better quality service for our clients. Many Thrive Group employees describe what they do as “more than just a job”. Teams work collaboratively to enhance the lives of the people we serve and that creates a strong sense of satisfaction and pride.
For the past two years, Thrive Group has been certified as a GREAT WORKPLACE after a thorough and independent analysis conducted by Great Place to Work® Canada. This certification is based on direct feedback from employees, provided as part of an extensive and anonymous survey about the workplace experience.

If this sounds like the kind of organization you would like to be a part of, check out our job listings below. You can view postings for all Thrive Group organizations based on your skills and areas of interest.

Who We Are

St. Peter’s is a not for profit organization, operated by Thrive Group. Thrive Group has been created to bring like-minded organizations together to develop a broad range and continuum of services that enable clients to live fulfilling lives as independently as possible.

Who We Hire

At Thrive Group organizations, workplace culture matters. We believe that providing our employees with a supportive, healthy work environment means better quality service for our clients. Many Thrive Group employees describe what they do as “more than just a job”. Teams work collaboratively to enhance the lives of the people we serve and that creates a strong sense of satisfaction and pride.
For the past two years, Thrive Group has been certified as a GREAT WORKPLACE after a thorough and independent analysis conducted by Great Place to Work® Canada. This certification is based on direct feedback from employees, provided as part of an extensive and anonymous survey about the workplace experience.

Come Join Bruce Grey Child and Family Services!

BGCFS is committed to staffing a workforce representative of the diverse populations we serve. We encourage applicants from Indigenous, African Canadian, people with disabilities, 2SLGBTQ+ communities to apply and self identify in their cover letter. We are committed to creating an accessible environment for all. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

BGCFS has its main office in Owen Sound and an additional office in Walkerton. Bruce and Grey counties offer many kilometers of waterfront along Lake Huron and Georgian Bay. These two bodies of water are central attractions for the year round and seasonal residents. We are also a close drive to the Blue Mountains area.

BGCFS is committed to partnering with the communities to provide professional child welfare services, focused on promoting the best interests, protection, and well-being of children and youth. We work with parents, family members, and community organizations to promote and support families.
We are committed to equity, diversity, and inclusion in our workplace and our community. Click here to learn more.

Why Work With Us
We’re making a difference in the lives of adult learners and the practitioners that serve them every year, and you’ll be an integral part of making that happen.
We take professional development seriously. During your time at ABC Life Literacy Canada, we’ll work with you to grow your skills and expertise, whether that’s through coaching, coursework, mentorship, or new project opportunities.
We know that our work is important, but that our families are more important, and we support work-life balance.
We value rest. Our office closes between Christmas and New Years, and we have half-day Fridays in July and August.

ABC Life Literacy Canada is a national charitable organization that champions literacy by empowering adult learners, raising awareness, and supporting the adult learning sector. We envision a Canada where everyone has the skills and opportunities to fully participate in learning, life, and work.

Dental Careers in Ottawa
Looking for a rewarding career in a modern Ottawa dental practice?

About Our Ottawa Practice

Dentistry On Catherine Street (DOCS) is a premier multidisciplinary dental specialty practice located in downtown Ottawa, Ontario. We are proud to deliver expert oral maxillofacial surgery, periodontal treatments, endodontic services, and dental implant therapy to the National Capital Region. Our dedicated team provides patients with exceptional dental care through compassion, technical excellence, and the use of advanced clinical technology. While we were previously known as GumDocs, our new brand, DOCS, continues to uphold the highest standards of specialized dental service in Ottawa.

Our success is built on a foundation of teamwork and professional excellence. We believe that every employee plays a vital role in our office culture and patient outcomes. We are committed to the personal and professional development of our staff, fostering an environment where every team member works toward the same goal: providing Ottawa’s best dental experience.

We are committed to being a living wage employer, and are certified with the Ontario Living Wage Network. We are a Christian not-for-profit senior living community experienced in delivering distinctive services within a continuum of living. Our mandate is to empower elders to live well through excellence, community, and grace.

If you are looking for a place to contribute to the holistic health and wellness of our elders and belong to a highly valued staff team in a stable industry, consider applying with Christie Gardens.

We care about the well-being of our elders — not only physically, but emotionally and spiritually as well. We have high standards for our home and facility, making us a respected leader in the industry for over 40 years.

Our Staff

We encourage diversity, inclusion, transparency, the sharing of new ideas, well-being, and work-life balance.

Kiss The Cook

For most families, the kitchen is the heart of the home and it’s no different at Porteous Lodge.  On any given day, it’s not unusual to find some of the residents asking if they can help to get the next meal on the table. 

“They peel potatoes, peel carrots.  They chop, they dice,” says Carmene Doucette.  “I try to get them involved as much as I can.” 

Carmene Doucette is the head cook at Porteous.  She’s been the head cook for eleven years.  But her attempts to provide meaningful work in the kitchen for some of the residents have uncovered a few flaws.  The kitchen is small and there isn’t a proper workspace for the residents.  She’s hoping that will soon change. 

“I’ve asked for that on my wish list; a kitchen area for them with low counters where wheelchairs can fit underneath and low sinks where they can wash their dishes,” says Carmene.

At Community Living Oakville, we want to ensure a transparent and fair application process for all. That's why we DO NOT require an application fee at any stage of our hiring process. To apply for a position, please complete the submission form by clicking “READ MORE”. If you have any concerns or encounter unauthorized requests for payments, please reach out to Carrie Moses, our HR Generalist, at carrie.moses@oakcl.org We're here to support you throughout your application journey.

The Working Centre is a dynamic community based organization.

Co-workers, volunteers and members of the community come from all walks of life, different cultural backgrounds, and an eclectic mix of age groups. 

Volunteering is a valued way of being part of the Working Centre Team. It is also, an ideal way for us to get to know you and for you to get to know The Working Centre.

Employment at The Working Centre offers flexible yet anchored work roles and an environment where meaningful, respectful and good work is a priority.

The Working Centre has an equal salary policy across the organization, reducing comparisons and hierarchy. Our leadership has a history of accepting less money in respect of this ethic.

This work is more than a job – it is about committed community work in a collaborative environment. If you think that this role would make you excited to come to work every day, we’d like to hear from you.

Feel welcome to send your resume and a detailed cover letter expressing your interest in working for The Working Centre, to working@theworkingcentre.org; or respond to one of our posted positions.

Children’s Aid Foundation of Canada’s Team Values

We’re dedicated to empowering children and youth to realize brighter futures through our five ‘VOICE’ values:

Vital Learning and Innovation – Trying new things, creating space and time for creativity and professional development, showing humility, cross-team collaboration, and being intentional about change.

Open Communication – Showing respect for others’ ideas and different ways of communicating, creating safe spaces, encouraging participation, building a pervasive practice of giving and receiving feedback, and active listening.

Inclusion and Diversity – Being welcoming, building a diverse team that represents those we serve, showing respect and appreciation of boundaries, seeking diverse perspectives, and adopting an equity and fairness lens.

Care and Compassion – Exercising self-care, empathy for others, showing compassion for our cause and purpose, respecting and encouraging work-life balance.

Empowering Accountability – Taking the initiative to improve and optimize, transparency and sharing information, clarifying expectations, tracking goals, and communicating them.

We need your gifts at ERDO!

Do you have a passion for Christian humanitarian ministry and want to be part of incredible life-transforming work?

ERDO (Emergency Relief and Development Overseas) is a registered Canadian charity that serves as the humanitarian agency of the Pentecostal Assemblies of Canada (PAOC). Register Today
Connect2Work for Newcomers
Program Description

At YMCA BC, we believe every newcomer youth deserves a strong start to their career journey in Canada. Connect2Work program is dedicated to bridging the gap between settlement and employment, offering the essential skills, coaching and connections you need to confidently build your future in the local job market.

Drop-in
Free
Group of 15-20
Y Membership Required: No
Connect2Work for Newcomers
Program overview

Connect2Work is a free, pre-employment program that supports newcomer youth (ages 15 to 30) and adults (30+) in developing essential life and job readiness skills. Participants take part in workshops, receive one-on-one employment coaching, build communication and English language skills, digital skills and gain access to networking opportunities.

This program is available at the Robert Lee YCMA centre in downtown Vancouver.
Program benefits
Weekly skill-building workshops led by experienced facilitators
Individual coaching to navigate resumes, interviews and Canadian workplace culture
ESL support to strengthen communication and workplace readiness
Networking opportunities with peers and local professionals
Supportive environment that encourages settlement confidence and self-advocacy
Program eligibility
Ages 15+
Must be a newcomer

At Catholic Crosscultural Services (CCS), we are passionate about helping newcomers thrive. Our team is made up of dedicated, compassionate individuals who work together to support our community and make a real impact in the lives of others.

Join Our Team

Located in the heart of the city, Saint Vincent’s Nursing Home is an accredited long-term care facility with 146 beds. It’s an organization that operates under the Care by Design physician-per-floor model. We value work-life balance, and at Saint Vincent’s, you will find a strong sense of community, commitment, and involvement. As a valued member of our team, you will have the opportunity to actively contribute and make a difference in the lives of our community.

If you value safety, diversity and inclusion, and are looking for an employer where respect, accountability, and professional development are valued, please take a moment to peruse our job vacancies. If you wish to apply for any current vacancy, please complete an Application for Employment (external), include the competition number, and submit it together with your resume to employment@svnh.ca or leave it with the receptionist. You can make a difference in the lives of our residents.

Why join The Kettle Society?
The Kettle Society is looking for those that are passionate about mental health!  Those that want to make a positive difference in the lives of people living with mental illness, poverty, and homelessness. We value safety.

We value a client-centered approach.

We value a non-judgmental, respectful and compassionate approach.

We value every person’s right to the dignity of having a home.

We value professional integrity and consistency.

Extended Medical and Dental (employer-paid for Part and Full Time employees)

Long Term Disability

About Onward
Onward offers a career that is meaningful

As one of the largest and fastest-growing not-for-profit affordable housing providers in the city, we house more than 3,000 people in our more than 30 buildings. 

As a member of our Onward team, you are making a very real and tangible difference in the lives of individuals - vulnerable people with limited resources to help themselves. We welcome individuals, families, seniors, Indigenous persons, newcomers to Canada, and others. We work with more than 60 social service agencies who refer and support our residents. To do this well takes an exceptional team of employees who value positive results, show leadership, and believe in the non-judgmental inclusion of others.

If this sounds like you, we invite you to consider joining us. We are always looking for great talent, please submit your resume or expression of interest to: joinus@onwardhomes.ca

Building services

The Building Services team at Onward plays a crucial role in ensuring the optimal functioning and upkeep of our properties.

At Umbrella Family, our people are at the heart of everything we do. Across 27 locations in the greater Hamilton area, our teams create nurturing, play-based learning environments where children – and educators – can thrive. We are committed to providing responsive, accessible, and inclusive child care programs for infants, toddlers, preschoolers, and school-aged children.

Careers

Gateway Resources is proud to employ a group of thoughtful, compassionate professionals that help to assist our Residents and Participants enjoy an enhanced quality of life.

Gateway offers a competitive wage structure, excellent health and dental benefits (100% of the premiums paid by the employer) and a matched contribution pension plan.

If you think that you would enjoy working in a hands-on environment where you are able to make a positive difference in the lives of others, then you might be a great candidate to be a Direct Support Professional.

View jobs at Gateway Resources Inc

Frequently Asked Questions by Direct Support Professionals (DSP’s)

1. What type of training do I need to be a DSP?
    Minimum Grade 10 education; valid Driver’s License; CPR/First Aid Training upon hire; experience in the health care field is an asset.

2. Are there opportunities for growth in my career?
    Yes, there are management positions in all divisions to work towards.

3. Are the hours flexible?
    Yes. We have part time, full time, and casual positions available. Positions in residential are set on a 2-week rotation      basis. Options available include daytime, evening, and overnight shifts. All residential staff work every other                  weekend. There is also opportunity to pick up extra hours.

Career Opportunities
Career Opportunities at Ronald McDonald House® Alberta

We have exciting career opportunities for those looking to make an impact on the lives of families that call the Ronald McDonald House their home while their seriously ill or injured children receive care in an Alberta hospital.

We are currently engaged in a multi-year, $80M capital campaign to reduce the service gap by tripling our capacity in Calgary and Edmonton. Construction on an expanded facility in Calgary completed in May 2025, increasing the number of suites from 27 to 91. The expanded Calgary facility is 105,000 square feet, offering family suites and a multitude of common areas that enhance the family experience within our communal living environment. Tentatively Edmonton will expand from 36 suites to approximately 100 suites. The expansion critical path in its entirety is 5-7 years.

In addition to serving more families, the expansion creates more volunteer opportunities as well as part-time and full-time employment opportunities where you can be a part of making a difference in the lives of the families we serve. See below for positions we are currently hiring for.

COMMUNITY PROGRAMS
HOW WE GIVE
Sport is proven to be a powerful tool in helping young people reach their potential. With the support of our teams and community partners, these quality sport programs are delivered at no cost to youth across the city. Our community programs bring people together. They create employment opportunities for local youth leaders. They give youth a chance to get in the game.
1
READY TO REBOUND

In partnership with the Toronto Raptors and Sun Life, Ready to Rebound brings organized basketball to communities facing barriers at no cost. This non-competitive basketball program is designed for youth ages 8-14. Ready to Rebound reaches youth at the community level offering a chance to learn the basics of basketball while discovering life skills through sport, taught by trained coaches.
2
HOME ICE HOCKEY

Home Ice Hockey brings non-competitive organized hockey to youth facing barriers at no cost. In partnership with Securian Canada, the Toronto Maple Leafs, Toronto Maple Leafs Alumni Association, and the City of Toronto, Home Ice Hockey activates 13 community sites, including two girls-only locations, and provides protective equipment with on-ice instruction to 350 young hockey enthusiasts. The program operates every weekend from September through March offering youth a chance to learn the basics of hockey while discovering life skills through sport from trained coaches.

KICKSTART SOCCER

Thank you for considering Main Street Project as your next career destination!

Main Street Project (MSP) is a community health centre with a mandate to address the social determinants of health which give rise to issues of addiction, physical and mental health issues and homelessness. MSP uses housing-first and harm reduction principles in the provision of a safe, respectful and accessible place for individuals who are homeless or at risk of homelessness in the community. Since 1972, the organization has been providing a range of services to Winnipeg’s most marginalized residents, including emergency shelter and nutrition services, drug and alcohol withdrawal management services, health advocacy and support, case management services, housing support services, food bank services and a mobile community outreach program.

Are you looking for a career where you will be part of a strong, diverse team of people, and where the work is rooted in the vision of every individual having access to a safe space where dignity, respect and self-determination are supported? Main Street Project is dedicated to creating meaningful and impactful solutions that contribute to positive change in our community. Your work here will have a lasting and meaningful impact on those we serve.

Thanks for your interest in employment on the Springs Team!

Springs operates differently than many churches in that we distinguish very little difference between paid staff positions and volunteer positions. In fact, many of our volunteers are ministry leaders, but with unpaid positions. When we determine that a ministry role can only be filled by a paid staff person, we look to those people already plugged in and working on the Springs Team. This gives us a track record of the person already passionately labouring alongside us.

Please note: Springs keeps resumes on file for 3 months and while we appreciate your application, only those applicants that are being considered will be contacted.

To express an interest in working at Springs, please submit your resume along with a cover letter that clearly indicates your area of employment interest and specifically where you are currently volunteering at Springs to:

hr@springschurch.com or, mail to our mailing address

The benefits

We also offer free onsite parking, all 13 current government statutory holidays as paid days off, and everyone starts at 3 weeks vacation (pro-rated). FearIsNotLove (formerly the Calgary Women’s Emergency Shelter) is a non-profit agency focused on ending family violence and abuse in the lives of women, children, youth and men.  We are committed to creating a safe and stable environment for our clients and an environment where our employees can thrive. We believe that every individual brings their own talent and strengths contributing directly to our growth and success. 

 

We continue to evolve and are looking for people who want to grow with us.  If you embrace change and new opportunities we want you on our team. We take pride in not only nurturing healthy families through our intervention and prevention programs but also through our employee programs:

 

Competitive comparable salary packages with benefits, matching RRSP and generous paid time off allowances
Employee & Family Assistance Program
Professional training and learning programs
Flexible & Alternate work arrangements

 

We are looking for people who are open to change, possibility and lead by example with a positive and supportive communication style.

 

We seek counsellors who are comfortable with client-centred approaches and who are familiar with the response-based approach (Dr. Allan Wade and Dr. Linda Coates) and Feedback Informed Treatment (Dr. Scott Miller).

Full-Time Psychologist or Provisional Psychologist

Society for Autism Support and Services requires an enthusiastic and motivated Psychologist or Provisional Psychologist to fill a full-time permanent position.

The start date is August 26, 2026.

As a member of our Psychology Department, you will provide consultative services to children, youth, families, and program staff across our Out of Home Living, School Age, and Early Support programs. Core responsibilities include:

Developing intervention strategies to address disruptive or unsafe behaviours
Delivering individual and group programming
Facilitating the development of self-help, play, social, and cognitive/academic skills within a multidisciplinary team
Completing comprehensive psychological assessments (i.e., cognitive, academic, achievement, and diagnostic assessments); training can be provided
Conducting ongoing training for parents, agency staff, and others regarding the principles, rationale, and strategies necessary to address specific behavioural and developmental concerns
Supervising or mentoring Behavioural Consultants in their work

The role involves work in diverse settings (family homes, community locations, SASS preschool, and our Out of Home Living Program) and requires flexible hours (e.g., daytimes, after-school, some evenings). Access to a personal vehicle is required.

Join Our Team

 

Primary Support Workers:
The Primary Support Worker is a team leader, assisting and coordinating efficient supports and the Person-Centered Plan. Empowering people to be active in their community and live the life they choose. Provide a high standard of quality care including daily living supports, medical, family needs, recreational, financial and over all wellbeing while working closely with their team and in-home manager.

 

Support Workers:

Join the Trans Canada Trail team!
 
Join us in continually improving the longest network of multi-use recreational trails in the world.
Who we are

The Trans Canada Trail (the Trail) is the longest inter-connected network of multi-use recreational trails in the world, spanning over 29,000 kilometres on land and water. Linking three oceans – the Atlantic, Pacific and Arctic – the Trail connects 900 municipalities across every province and territory. It is a ribbon that connects Canada’s diverse landscapes, seasons, people and experiences, and fosters unity, collaboration and connectedness.

Trans Canada Trail (the organization) is a registered charity that stewards this national trail in collaboration with community-based partners. With funding from the Government of Canada through Parks Canada, and investments from all levels of government and generous donors, Trans Canada Trail is the largest investor in trail infrastructure projects in Canada, supporting improvements, growth and enhancements for the benefit of generations to come.

Bridges of Canada has provided multi-faith chaplaincy services to Correctional Service Canada (CSC) since 2016, supporting the spiritual care and rehabilitation of individuals in federal institutions across Canada.

​

Through partnerships with faith communities nationwide, Bridges supports a broad range of spiritual traditions including Protestant, Roman Catholic, Indigenous spiritual practices, Muslim, Sikh, Jewish, Buddhist, Hindu, Wiccan, Rastafarian, and other faith groups. These services contribute to offender rehabilitation, institutional well-being, and successful reintegration into the community.

​

Bridges of Canada has also partnered with CSC on Faith Community Reintegration Projects (FCRP), helping support individuals transitioning from incarceration back into the community through faith-based mentorship, programming, and community support networks.

​

In Alberta, Bridges additionally provides provincial chaplaincy coordination services across correctional and remand facilities, helping ensure consistent access to spiritual and religious care for incarcerated individuals of all faith backgrounds.

Why Lead?

“Lead helps children and their families show the world what they can do.”

Our dynamic teams provide opportunities for children and families to learn through specialized programs in their home and community.  We support children and their families believing children learn best through play and within naturally occurring day-to-day activities.  Using an “I do, we do, you do” model for learning, we work through the stages of skill development together with children, families, and community partners.  Together, we develop strong, flexible programs.

We take pride in our positive workplace culture that is preserved through an environment of respect, collaboration, creativity and wellness.  This forms the foundation of the excellent work we do with families, children, community partners and each other.  We value what you will bring to our team and we want to be part of your professional journey.

For 56 years, Aventa Addiction Treatment for Women has been offering alcohol, drug, gambling and nicotine addiction treatment for women.

Aventa offers a trauma-informed curriculum specifically designed for women with addictions, the treatment program has been researched and written by Dr. Stephanie Covington, a nationally recognized clinician, author and lecturer. Our treatment programs place an emphasis on providing an environment that is safe, supportive, and establishes a clear recovery process for women.

One of Aventa’s priorities is working with pregnant women; pregnant women are admitted without delay which helps decrease the risks associated with Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD).

Aventa is Accredited with Exemplary Standing from Accreditation Canada.

Aventa values diversity and is committed to employment equity in recruiting Aventa Board Members, Staff, and Volunteers.

Megan Acres - Rural Healthcare: The Next Generation

 

Investing in the Next Generation of Rural Healthcare

Over the past year, Community Foundation Grey Bruce Executive Director Stuart Reid has had the privilege of mentoring University of Western Ontario master's candidate Megan Acres as she completed her capstone project, Rural Healthcare: The Next Generation.

A lifelong resident of Meaford and graduate of Georgian Bay Community School, Megan has brought both academic expertise and personal insight to a research project focused on an important local challenge: how to encourage more Grey-Bruce students to pursue careers in healthcare. Using an equity lens, Megan's research examines the barriers and protective factors that influence whether students from Grey-Bruce pursue post-secondary education in healthcare fields. Her findings highlight several key factors that shape student decisions, including hands-on learning opportunities, financial considerations, transportation challenges, self-selection, and access to professional networks and mentorship (see graphic below).

The Comox Valley Community Foundation has served the Comox Valley since 1996. We work to bring together the generosity of donors with the ideas and energy of charities and communities across the Comox Valley. Join us and start making a difference in our community.

Why the Comox Valley?

The Comox Valley is considered one of the most desirable places to live in Canada due to its incredible natural setting on the Salish Sea, mild climate and limitless year-round recreational pursuits. An outdoor adventure and culinary destination, you can ski with an ocean view, swim in crystal clear waters, mountain bike or hike along one of the many river systems, paddle the many kilometres of stunning shoreline, or tee off at one of many scenic golf courses. The Comox Valley is also renowned for its many cultural, art, music, and food festivals.

MUST LOVE CATS

We are seeking passionate individuals who share MEOW Foundation’s goals, mission and vision.

Our Vision
All communities caring for every cat with respect and compassion.

Our Mission
Champion the well-being of cats through humane programs, advocacy and education.

Our Goals

Work with the public to care for and provide shelter to stray, abandoned and feral cats in Calgary;
Provide the highest level of care to every cat who enters our programs and immediate veterinary treatment to sick or injured cats;
Match adoptable cats with loving families who will provide them with care and attention for the rest of their natural lives;
Promote responsible pet ownership, including the spaying/neutering of all cats and kittens; provision of nutritious food, water, shelter, love, regular health care and permanent identification such as a microchip or tattoo; licensing; and prevention from becoming a nuisance;
Reduce feline overpopulation and suffering;
Promote humane treatment, compassion and respect for all animals.
WHY WORK AT MEOW FOUNDATION?
We offer many benefits and perks including a worthwhile opportunity to work in the non-profit sector and feel a sense of personal fulfillment in the area of animal welfare
Work with a caring and knowledgeable team of staff and volunteers

Free parking

THE CYCLE STOPS WHEN WE START TO HEAL TOGETHER

Join Our Journey of Healing: NohKohWaa Family and Youth Centre is Hiring!

NohKohWaa is a 24-7 Residential Family & Youth Centre in Fort Macleod. NohKohWaa recognizes the impact colonization has had and intends to realign and reconnect with our true Indigenous nature supported by those who truly understand from their own lived experience. A home is not made with bricks and mortar or wood and nails; a home is made with love, kindness, and respect. We could live in any building, anywhere in the world but if love is absent, happiness will evade us. NohKohWaa believes a home is where every child is valued and knows their worth.

Why Join Us:

Make a meaningful impact: Contribute to a national movement focused on improving wellness for all people.
Holistic approach: Be part of an organization that addresses the whole person and respects individual needs.
Inclusive community: Work alongside diverse and passionate individuals committed to positive change.
Embrace tradition: Support the revival of Indigenous traditional knowledge and values in the healing process.

If you are driven by compassion, inspired by cultural diversity, and dedicated to fostering positive change, we invite you to be a part of our dynamic team. Join us in building a community where healing from the heart is not just a goal but a way of life.

At the Mental Health Foundation, we believe a diverse and inclusive work environment is essential to our success.
Our values and mission statement are rooted in the idea that everyone deserves to be treated with respect and dignity, and diversity is a strength to be celebrated.

An inclusive workplace isn’t just the right thing to do, it brings a wide range of perspectives, experiences, and ideas to the table, helping us to better understand our donors more effectively. It also attracts and retains the best talent, because our employees know they are valued for who they are, and that their background and identity are embraced.

Creating an inclusive workplace isn’t just about policies and procedures—it’s about creating a culture that values and respects diversity. We strive to foster a welcoming and supportive environment where everyone feels safe, seen, and heard. That means actively listening to our team, providing opportunities for professional development and growth, and celebrating the unique talents and contributions of each individual.

We also recognize there is always more work to be done, and we are committed to ongoing learning and growth in the areas of diversity, equity, and inclusion. We believe that by working together, we can create a truly inclusive, accessible, and enriching workplace for everyone.
