We are a group of idealistic believers in education for all. We look for talented, forward-thinking and energetic individuals to join our team. Be a part of TCF and shape the future!

JOB TITLE: Chief Executive Officer (CEO)

ORGANIZATION: The Citizens Foundation Canada (TCF Canada)
LOCATION: Oakville, Ontario 

ORGANIZATIONAL OVERVIEW

About The Citizens Foundation Canada
The Citizens Foundation Canada (TCF Canada) is a CRA-registered charity that supports The Citizens Foundation’s (TCF’s) mission to provide quality education for underprivileged children in Pakistan. Through fundraising, advocacy, and community engagement across Canada, TCF Canada works to help build and support schools in underprivileged communities. By focusing on providing an enabling environment for students—especially girls—TCF Canada believes in breaking the cycle of poverty through education.  Over the past 30 years, TCF has become one of Pakistan’s leading organizations in the field of education for the less privileged, operating over 2,000 school units and serving over 300,000 students.  Learn more at tcfcanada.org.

ScreenRant is your watercooler. It’s your movie theater foyer. It’s your comic book shop. It’s that friend with uncontrollable passion - and the knowledge and insight to back it up. We want to discuss the things we’re passionate about in an authoritative, informed, professional way, matching our readers’ excitement for what’s currently happening in the multiverse of entertainment.

Our passion is matched with industry expertise and connections. We’ve broken major stories, like Avengers: Endgame’s record-busting return to theaters. We’ve been on the set of more major movies than we can count. And we’ve hosted headline-dominating events like Star Wars Celebration’s Mandalorian panel and Marvel’s massive SDCC Hall H panel revealing Phase 5 & 6. We’ve been the first to spot incredible Easter eggs in franchises like Star Wars and Star Trek, and celebrate smaller movies, shows and games that deserve a wider audience. Careers in Quantum

A new industry is being built in quantum computing.    

Today, Canada is a world leader in quantum computing and there is a chronic shortage of quantum talent. The quantum industry will create well-paid, interesting jobs with opportunities for people across a wide range of knowledge and skill sets.

The Quantum Careers Initiative aims to help you find your place in the quantum ecosystem in Canada. 

Visit the Quantum Careers Initiative
People with related skill sets can transition to a career in the quantum industry. 

 McKinsey data from 2021 showed that for every person with a Masters qualification in quantum technology, there are close to three available jobs. 

To grow, the quantum industry will need people with STEM and technical skills, as well as tech savvy business and commercialization experts. Quantum ethicists, sustainability experts, and EDI professionals will also be needed for this new industry. 

Math, statistics, physics, chemistry, biochemistry, computer science, engineering and business are all fields related to quantum.

People & Careers
Our Purpose 

To provide leadership and create opportunities for profitable growth for Saskatchewan pulses. 

When you work at SPG, you are working on behalf of the over 15,000 pulse farmers in Saskatchewan. As a team we strive to make the industry more innovative, sustainable, and profitable for producers and the local economy. SPG carries out work in the areas of research and development, agronomy extension, market development, grower communications, and market access and advocacy.  

Our Culture 

Our team members are empowered to lead work in their respective areas of expertise with support and encouragement from leaders. Employees at all levels lead the development of annual workplans and are accountable for the execution of that work. As a relatively small organization, SPG can be nimble and flexible to adapt and change. Employees say that they have a clear line of sight to how their work has a direct impact for farmers. 

The main pillars to SPG’s culture include: 

A common sense of purpose – guides why we do what we do 
Achievement – focused on organizational and team goals 
Humanistic – supportive and helpful of others 
Growth mindset – helping employees grow  
Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion 

At SPG, we see diversity as an asset and strive to make our work and our organization inclusive.

About Us

Sport for Life is a Victoria-based non-profit that aims to mobilize quality sport and physical literacy development to the world. It is recognized as the global experts on the Canadian Sport for Life Movement, Long-Term Development in Sport and Physical Activity, and physical literacy development. By bridging gaps between sectors, creating new collaborations with Canadian institutions, and mobilizing our knowledge to communities across the country, we aim to create a future in which absolutely everyone has access to quality sport and physical literacy experiences.

Sport for Life strives to be a catalyst for change and aspires to create equitable, diverse, inclusive, and accessible opportunities for everyone not only in sport and physical activity, but also in the workplace. Our talented and diverse team, which is spread out across Canada, is dedicated to a common vision and a set of shared core values. Our workspace is inclusive, friendly and team-based, and we walk our talk by enabling our employees to live the most active lives possible.

We love to bake for you, greet you and treat you. Thank you for your support.

Our Story

We are a husband and wife team baking our dreams.  Our names are Isaac and Myroslava. We romanticized about opening up our own bakery and café when we first met in 2010. At that time, Isaac was working as a ceramicist and Myro was baking bread at a bakery in the downtown area. We loved our simple life but we wanted to do more. We wanted to find a way to channel both of our passions into meaningful work that was rooted in quality, passion and most importantly community. After years of dreaming, tears, and sweat, we finally opened up shop in 2015. Our family has expanded as we now have three beautiful children by our side, and an amazing group of like minded individuals that we have the honour of calling our friends and our teammates. We could not be happier sharing our passion with you.

ARTWORKSTO: YOUTH PROGRAM
ABOUT THE PROGRAM

The ArtWorksTO Youth Program supports Indigenous, Black, and People of Colour (IBPOC) youth media creatives (ages 18–29) who are pursuing careers in the media arts industry. As a workforce development program, it provides opportunities to gain professional experience through paid creative contract work, while also offering industry-specific mentorship, skills training, and access to networks.

WHAT DOES THE PROGRAM OFFER?

ArtWorksTO Youth Program provides: 

OCAD U led Portfolio Development Workshops for prospective applicants 
OCAD U Certificate in Art and Design Professional Skills or two mandatory OCAD U courses 
A guaranteed, paid creative contract valued at $5,000 
Group and one-on-one mentorship with industry professionals 
Networking through masterclasses, workshops and panels 
Wrap-around supports 
Upon completion of the program, access to the ArtWorksTO Alumni Pathways Program 
ELIGIBILITY & SELECTION CRITERIA

ArtWorksTO Youth Program receives many strong applications each year. Meeting the eligibility requirements does not guarantee acceptance.

MACHINE PERCEPTION ENGINEERING — ONTARIO, CANADA

AI has already formed an opinion about your business.

Not your content. Not your branding. The structured signal layer that AI reads before anything else — and the one most businesses have never touched.

SEE HOW AI PERCEIVES YOU NOW

THE GAP

When someone asks AI for a family lawyer in Mississauga, a financial adviser in Burlington, or a medspa in Oakville, the results aren't ranked by quality. They're ranked by entity coherence — how clearly and consistently the machine can classify what a business is, who runs it, and whether it's credentialed. Most local professional service businesses are incoherent to AI without knowing it. That incoherence is invisible. And it's costing referrals to businesses that simply have cleaner structured signals.

WHAT WE DO

01
Audit how AI currently perceives your business

We run the queries your potential clients are actually asking — across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and others — and document exactly how AI classifies your entity today. Most businesses have never seen this.

02
Correct the signals causing misclassification

We work at the structured data layer: NAP consistency, JSON-LD schema, credential linking, directory signal coherence. Under the hood. Nothing that touches your brand or your content.

03
Measure your retrieval share — and move it

We are a hyper-growth biotechnology company enabling viral medicines that improve the lives of people. We own proprietary small molecules called Viral Sensitizers (VSE™) that target the natural antiviral defenses of cells to boost virus manufacturing yields (e.g., vaccines) and improve the effectiveness of therapeutics that rely on viral vectors (e.g., gene therapy).
